review_summary: Transcript is sufficient for stacked RNN structure and compact notation. The relationships across layer depth and time are stated verbally.

question_bank.question: In a stacked RNN, what does an upper recurrent layer at time t typically receive as input?
choices: Only its own previous hidden state
The lower layer's output at the same time step
The final output token of the whole sequence
A fresh copy of the raw input sequence
question_bank.explanation: Stacking means higher layers process representations produced by lower layers, while still keeping their own recurrent connections through time.

transcript_quotes: output y from the previous layer

question_bank.question: How are weights shared in a multilayer RNN?
choices: All layers and all time steps share one identical weight set
Weights are shared through time within a layer, but different layers have different weights
Weights are shared across layers but not across time
No weights are shared at all
question_bank.explanation: A recurrent layer reuses its own parameters across time, but a different stacked layer learns its own separate transformation.

question_bank.question: Why do authors sometimes combine W_hh and W_xh into one larger matrix in the notation?
choices: Because the hidden state and input are mathematically identical
Because the model stops distinguishing recurrent and input terms
As a shorthand after stacking vectors, even though the conceptual blocks are still distinct
To avoid using an activation function
question_bank.explanation: The compact form is mainly notational convenience. It bundles the linear terms together, but the recurrent contribution and input contribution still play different conceptual roles.
